featureName: BUG-039-004: price-drop watch threshold has a hardcoded 0.10 fallback (NO-DEFAULTS violation)
reporter: owner directive (no const limits) — autonomous open-items sweep

executionHistory.summary: Autonomous open-items sweep found a genuine hardcoded fallback the earlier triage under-classified as 'user-configured, low value': internal/recommendation/watch/evaluator.go gatherPriceDropCandidates used thresholdPct = 0.10 as the final fallback when neither trigger.Context[threshold_pct] nor watch.Filters[threshold_pct] was set. That is a binding smackerel-no-defaults / NO-const-limits violation. Classified as an OPERATIONAL default (the threshold is a user-config value with a trigger override; 0.10 is only the missing-input fallback), so the correct fix is fail-loud SST, NOT an LLM call (consistent with every operational floor/cap/window in the BUG-021-005..011 sweep). Verified the only price-drop integration test always supplies threshold_pct: 0.15 (both filter + trigger), so no test is coupled to the 0.10 literal.

Single bugfix-fastlane scope: add recommendations.watches.default_price_drop_threshold_pct as fail-loud SST (parseUnitFloat + >0 guard); thread through watch.Options -> Evaluator; extract resolvePriceDropThreshold(trigger, watch, defaultPct) for the trigger->filter->default precedence (unit-testable without a DB); wire cfg value in cmd/core; add setRequiredEnv key. Rejected the LLM alternative (would re-architect a certified user-config-first scalar-threshold model — over-engineering a fallback). SCN-039-004-01..02 authored.

internal/config/recommendations.go: RecommendationWatchesConfig.DefaultPriceDropThresholdPct + parseUnitFloat load + >0 guard. config/smackerel.yaml: recommendations.watches.default_price_drop_threshold_pct: 0.10. scripts/commands/config.sh: RECOMMENDATIONS_WATCHES_DEFAULT_PRICE_DROP_THRESHOLD_PCT (assignment + emit). internal/recommendation/watch/evaluator.go: Options + Evaluator field + NewEvaluator threading; new resolvePriceDropThreshold helper; gatherPriceDropCandidates calls it; 0.10 literal removed. cmd/core/wiring_recommendation_watches.go: pass cfg.Recommendations.Watches.DefaultPriceDropThresholdPct. internal/config/validate_test.go: setRequiredEnv key. New internal/recommendation/watch/threshold_test.go.

threshold_test.go: TestResolvePriceDropThreshold_Precedence (5 sub-cases: trigger wins; filter wins when trigger absent/zero; SST default when neither/both-zero) + TestResolvePriceDropThreshold_UsesConfiguredDefault (0.30 passthrough proves no baked-in 0.10). go build ./... + vet green. Fixed two authoring slips during test: a duplicate package clause and a WatchRecord literal needing the embedded WatchInput (Filters is a promoted field).

Full recommendation/watch + config + cmd/core packages green. ./smackerel.sh config generate resolves the key into dev.env (line 217); config-validate OK. validate_test.go setRequiredEnv updated so all existing config-load tests stay green. The price-drop integration test (explicit 0.15 threshold) and non-price watch tests are unaffected (verified no coupling to the literal).

Extracting resolvePriceDropThreshold both removes the inline literal AND makes the precedence unit-testable without a DB — a net clarity gain. The three-branch inline if-cascade collapses to one early-return helper.

The helper treats both zero and absent threshold_pct as unset (numericFromAny returns 0 for both), matching the prior inline behaviour exactly. The loader's >0 guard prevents a degenerate 0 default (every price would count as a drop). Existing Options callers that omit the field get Go's zero value, which is only read on the price-drop path that production always wires from SST.
No new external surface; no secret; the SST value is a numeric operator knob. No change to what is sent to providers or persisted. No framework files touched.
artifact-lint PASSED for the packet; config generate + build + vet + affected packages green. Evidence in report.md.

grep of '= 0.10' in evaluator.go = 0 (literal removed). No migration; config/generated gitignored + regenerated per run (config generate --env test carries the key into the test stack); no .github/bubbles changes; parent spec 039 planning artifacts untouched.

evaluator.go, recommendations.go, smackerel.yaml document the operational/business boundary and the fail-loud SST default. The packet records this as the completion of the owner's NO-const-limits sweep and the operational-vs-business classification rationale.

lockdownState.reason: bugfix-fastlane NO-DEFAULTS remediation; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: internal/recommendation/watch/evaluator.go used a hardcoded thresholdPct = 0.10 fallback for the price-drop threshold when neither the trigger nor the user's watch filter supplied threshold_pct — a binding smackerel-no-defaults / NO-const-limits violation.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Moved the fallback to fail-loud SST (recommendations.watches.default_price_drop_threshold_pct, range (0,1]); extracted resolvePriceDropThreshold for the trigger->filter->SST-default precedence; threaded the value through Options/Evaluator and wired it in cmd/core. The literal is removed; user/trigger thresholds still take precedence.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md Test/Validation/Audit Evidence; internal/recommendation/watch/evaluator.go; internal/config/recommendations.go; grep of '= 0.10' in evaluator.go = 0.
